title: test: Fix test race in 030-run
body:
This seems like a test race that appeared sometimes with crun on ppc64le:

https://openqa.opensuse.org/tests/5338518#external (click on `Show only failures`):

```
# Test messages # podman run --preserve-fds
# failure: 

tags: ci:parallel
(from function `bail-now' in file test/system/helpers.bash, line 187,
 from function `is' in file test/system/helpers.bash, line 1112,
 in test file test/system/030-run.bats, line 84)
  `is "$output" "$content" "container read input from fd 4"' failed

[08:22:48.845085215] # /usr/bin/podman run --rm -i --preserve-fds=2 quay.io/libpod/testimage:20241011 sh -c cat <&4
[08:22:49.560516399] 1u3PrTiBXHjtkNZdKzDN
process not running: No such process
#/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
#|     FAIL: container read input from fd 4
#| expected: '1u3PrTiBXHjtkNZdKzDN'
#|   actual: '1u3PrTiBXHjtkNZdKzDN'
#|         > 'process not running: No such process'
#\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
# [teardown]
```

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
None
```

comments.body: [NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *lsm5*, *ricardobranco777* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). > [08:22:48.845085215] # /usr/bin/podman run --rm -i --preserve-fds=2 quay.io/libpod/testimage:20241011 sh -c cat <&4
> [08:22:49.560516399] 1u3PrTiBXHjtkNZdKzDN
> process not running: No such process
> #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
> #|     FAIL: container read input from fd 4
> #| expected: '1u3PrTiBXHjtkNZdKzDN'
> #|   actual: '1u3PrTiBXHjtkNZdKzDN'
> #|         > 'process not running: No such process'
> #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

That does sounds like a legit bug in podman, we should not print spurious error output on cleanup as stdout/err must only be the container output.
https://github.com/containers/crun/blob/ce72cd914878999658f59693061e32babb7a5ebb/src/libcrun/linux.c#L6035-L6036

So I think there is some race here that needs a closer look, do you have a reproducer?

> So I think there is some race here that needs a closer look, do you have a reproducer?

https://openqa.opensuse.org/tests/5338518/file/podman-commands.txt

We've seen this only on the slower ppc64le arch.
